ingredients.item:
The key to success with this dish (mì căn rim chua ngọt) is its flavorful sweet-and-sour sauce. If you’re not a fan of seitan, feel free to use any other plant-based protein you prefer, such as vegan chicken tenders or chunks, or textured vegetable protein. Anything that has a chewy, springy texture to absorb the sauce will work.
SKILL LEVELBasic|YIELD4 servings
¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil
ingredients.original:
The key to success with this dish (mì căn rim chua ngọt) is its flavorful sweet-and-sour sauce. If you’re not a fan of seitan, feel free to use any other plant-based protein you prefer, such as vegan chicken tenders or chunks, or textured vegetable protein. Anything that has a chewy, springy texture to absorb the sauce will work.
SKILL LEVELBasic|YIELD4 servings
¼ cup (60 ml) vegetable oil

ingredients.item: 7 ounces (200 g) store-bought or homemade seitan or vegan protein of choice
ingredients.original: 7 ounces (200 g) store-bought or homemade seitan or vegan protein of choice

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon roasted sesame seeds, for garnish
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon roasted sesame seeds, for garnish

ingredients.item: 1. In a medium skillet, heat oil over low heat. When hot, add seitan and cook until golden brown on all sides (3 to 5 minutes). Remove and place on a paper towel–lined plate to drain and let cool. Cut into ½-inch (1 cm) thick slices.
ingredients.original: 1. In a medium skillet, heat oil over low heat. When hot, add seitan and cook until golden brown on all sides (3 to 5 minutes). Remove and place on a paper towel–lined plate to drain and let cool. Cut into ½-inch (1 cm) thick slices.

ingredients.item: 2. In a small bowl, whisk together vinegar, ketchup, brown sugar, stock powder, sesame oil, soy sauce, sriracha, and vegetarian oyster sauce (if using) until solids are dissolved.
ingredients.original: 2. In a small bowl, whisk together vinegar, ketchup, brown sugar, stock powder, sesame oil, soy sauce, sriracha, and vegetarian oyster sauce (if using) until solids are dissolved.

ingredients.item: 3. Simmer seitan slices, prepared sauce, and water in a large skillet over medium-low heat until sauce thickens (about 15 minutes). Remove from heat.
ingredients.original: 3. Simmer seitan slices, prepared sauce, and water in a large skillet over medium-low heat until sauce thickens (about 15 minutes). Remove from heat.

ingredients.item: 4. Transfer to a platter, dust with pepper, sprinkle with sesame seeds, and serve.
ingredients.original: 4. Transfer to a platter, dust with pepper, sprinkle with sesame seeds, and serve.
